skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
skill.summary: A structured approach for handling code review feedback with technical verification before implementation
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill file (SKILL.md) is a markdown documentation file containing instructional text, not executable code. Backticks detected are markdown code block delimiters, not shell command execution. No actual cryptographic operations, network calls, or command injection vectors exist in this skill.

content.user_title: Handle code review feedback with technical verification
content.value_statement: Code review requires technical evaluation, not emotional performance. This skill provides a structured pattern for verifying review feedback before implementation, ensuring technical correctness over social comfort.
actual_capabilities: Systematically evaluate code review feedback against codebase reality
Identify unclear items and request clarification before implementation
Push back on suggestions with technical reasoning when appropriate
Implement fixes one at a time with individual testing
Apply YAGNI principle to reject unnecessary feature additions
Handle feedback from both trusted partners and external reviewers differently
limitations: Requires access to codebase for verification steps
Cannot independently verify claims without investigation capability
Designed for technical feedback, not style preference disputes
Assumes reviewer feedback is provided in a parseable format

use_cases.title: Multi-item review response
use_cases.description: When receiving a list of 6+ feedback items, clarify unclear items first, then implement in priority order (blocking issues first), testing each change individually to catch regressions early

use_cases.title: External reviewer skepticism
use_cases.description: When external reviewers suggest changes without full context, verify technical correctness for your codebase, check for breaking changes, and push back with evidence when the suggestion is wrong

use_cases.title: YAGNI feature gatekeeping
use_cases.description: When reviewers suggest implementing features 'properly' with database, filters, and exports, grep the codebase first to confirm actual usage before adding unnecessary complexity

prompt_templates.title: Clarify unclear feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: I received code review feedback with items 1-6. I understand items 1, 2, 3, and 6, but items 4 and 5 are unclear. Before implementing anything, I need clarification on what exactly is expected for items 4 and 5.
prompt_templates.scenario: When any part of the feedback is ambiguous

prompt_templates.title: Verify external reviewer suggestion
prompt_templates.prompt: A reviewer suggested changing X to Y. Let me verify: Is this technically correct for this codebase? Does it break existing functionality? What was the reason for the current implementation? I'll check the code and tests before proceeding.
prompt_templates.scenario: When evaluating suggestions from external reviewers

prompt_templates.title: Push back with technical reasoning
prompt_templates.prompt: I understand your suggestion to do X, but this would break Y because of Z. The current implementation handles edge case A and B. If we change this, we need to also update C and D. Should I proceed with the full scope or keep the current approach?
prompt_templates.scenario: When a suggestion would cause problems

prompt_templates.title: YAGNI check for feature requests
prompt_templates.prompt: You suggested implementing X with full database support, date filters, and CSV export. I searched the codebase and found no callers of this endpoint. Should I remove it entirely per YAGNI, or is there usage I'm missing?
prompt_templates.scenario: When reviewer suggests over-engineering unused features

output_examples.output: Checking compatibility requirements... Build target is 10.15+, this API needs 13+. Current implementation has wrong bundle ID. Options: fix bundle ID or drop pre-13 support. Which do you prefer?

output_examples.input: Reviewer: Implement metrics tracking with database, date filters, CSV export
output_examples.output: Searched codebase - no code calls this endpoint. Recommend removal per YAGNI. Is there expected usage I should know about?
I understand items 1, 2, 3, and 6. I need clarification on items 4 and 5 before proceeding with implementation.

best_practices: Always clarify all unclear items before implementing any part of the feedback
Verify suggestions against actual codebase behavior, not just surface logic
Push back with specific technical reasoning, not defensiveness or emotion
anti_patterns: Saying 'You're absolutely right!' or 'Great point!' before verification
Implementing feedback immediately without checking codebase reality
Batch-implementing multiple items without testing each change individually

faq.question: What should I do when review feedback is unclear?
faq.answer: Stop immediately and ask for clarification on the unclear items before implementing anything. Items may be related, and partial understanding leads to wrong implementation.

faq.question: How do I push back on a reviewer's suggestion?
faq.answer: Use technical reasoning with specific evidence. Reference working tests, code behavior, or compatibility requirements. Ask specific questions rather than making defensive statements.

faq.question: What if the reviewer is right and I pushed back?
faq.answer: State the correction factually: 'You were right - I checked X and it does Y. Implementing now.' Avoid long apologies or over-explaining why you pushed back.

faq.question: Should I thank reviewers for their feedback?
faq.answer: No. Actions speak louder. Just fix the issue and show what changed. If you catch yourself writing 'Thanks', delete it and state the fix instead.

faq.question: How do I handle suggestions to implement features 'properly'?
faq.answer: First grep the codebase for actual usage. If nothing calls the endpoint or feature, suggest removal per YAGNI. Only implement fully if there is confirmed usage.

faq.question: What order should I implement multi-item feedback?
faq.answer: After clarifying all items, implement in this order: blocking issues first (breaks, security), then simple fixes (typos, imports), then complex fixes (refactoring, logic). Test each fix individually.
